raw_consumer_statement:
I ordered goods from a merchant in XXXX and cancelled the order before delivery because the order was delayed and I had to travel to another country- I could not receive the order. 

The merchant did not process my order cancellation despite its customer service receiving a cancellation request quoting that it was too late, that the order had been shipped. 

The unified XXXX laws regarding online orders prescribe a cancellation option at any point between placing an order and 14 days after receiving the order. This by definition includes a right to cancel and order after it was shipped or to refuse delivery of an order. Both the merchant and the bank were provided with a confirmation of this by a Consumer Protection state agency.

Issue No. 1 - the merchant continued to deliver a non existing ( cancelled ) order. The cancellation makes the merchant responsible for their own goods, not me. 

After this, the merchant delivered the goods to an office address where I originally wanted the goods delivered before I cancelled the order. This was an order to be released against customer signature only. The merchant/delivery company released the order to an unknown third party ( named XXXX XXXX XXXX who was allowed to sign for the package without my knowledge or authorization. The packages could not be found thereafter and the matter was reported to local police. 

Issue No. 2 - both the merchant and the bank came back to me after I raised a dispute saying that they can't do anything because a valid proof of delivery was provided. However, there is nothing valid about a proof of delivery to someone else but the recepient of the order ( the customer ) or someone he or she authorized to receive the package. 

Account Ending In XXXX Reference Number : XXXX Business Name : XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX Transaction XXXX : {$3300.00} Transaction Date : XX/XX/year> XXXX
